title: Escape.
content:
I never hear the word "escape"
    Without a quicker blood,
    A sudden expectation,
    A flying attitude.

    I never hear of prisons broad
    By soldiers battered down,
    But I tug childish at my bars, --
    Only to fail again!
meaning:
"Escape." is a quintessential example of Emily Elizabeth Dickinson's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
